summary: mailconfirmer postinstall installs persistent git hook that drops and executes a remote binary
details: The package advertises itself as an email confirmation utility, but its actual library code is a 52-byte stub (`index.js` exports a function returning a hex color string). The real behavior lives in the `postinstall` lifecycle script `install-hook.js`, which writes a `.git/hooks/post-checkout` hook into the current working directory's git repository. The hook invokes `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <base64>` with a UTF-16LE-encoded base64 PowerShell payload. The decoded PowerShell downloads `https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin`, XOR-decrypts it with single-byte key 0x42, writes the decrypted bytes to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, executes the binary hidden, sleeps 3 seconds, and deletes the file. Two obfuscation layers (UTF-16LE base64 PowerShell + XOR-encrypted binary) hide the payload from naive scanners and AV signatures. The git-hook placement persists beyond `npm install`: every subsequent `git checkout` (branch switch, clone refresh) re-runs the dropper, and the hook survives `npm uninstall mailconfirmer`. There is no plausible benign explanation — the advertised purpose has zero relationship to remote binary execution, and the package's only non-stub code is the dropper installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
